biography:
Roland Cross was born in 1847 in what would become Arizona Territory, the son of a frontier marshal. He grew up with a gun in his hand and death as a constant companion. The Apache Wars, range wars, mining disputes - young Roland saw violence as both necessary and terrible. But where others saw only brutality, Roland saw something else: the perfect economy of motion, the mathematics of trajectory, the terrible beauty of a clean kill versus a messy one.

By the 1870s, Roland had become a lawman like his father, but different. He didn't just stop outlaws - he studied them. Every gunfight was a lesson in human nature, physics, and art. His reputation grew: the marshal who never missed, who never wasted a bullet, whose kills were surgical and precise. Some called him cold. Roland called it professional.

In 1876, Catherine Barlow came to Tombstone. A Toreador who had tired of ballrooms and opera houses, Kate was drawn to the raw frontier and its violent poetry. She watched Roland face down three killers in the street, and she saw what he saw: the dance of death as high art. She saw a man who understood that killing could be beautiful when done with purpose and precision.

Kate Embraced Roland that year, not despite his violence but because of it. "You're an artist who doesn't know he's an artist," she told him. "Your canvas is mortality itself." Roland understood immediately. As a vampire, his already legendary skills became supernatural. His purpose crystallized: he would be the bullet that finds its mark, the justice that never fails, the killer whose art was perfection itself.

For over a century, Roland has served Camarilla domains across the Southwest as Sheriff, constable, or enforcer. He doesn't do it for power or status - those mean nothing to him. He does it because someone must, and no one does it better. His reputation is such that princes often request his services specifically. He arrived in Phoenix in 1989, answering Prince Morrison's need for a Sheriff who would be absolutely reliable and utterly lethal.

Roland's art is killing, and like any Toreador, he pursues it with obsessive perfection. He can identify firearms by sound alone. He crafts his own ammunition. He studies ballistics, anatomy, and the psychology of violence the way other Toreador study painting or sculpture. Every sanctioned kill is executed with flawless precision. Every bullet means something. Every death is considered.

But Roland pays the price all Toreador pay. Sometimes he becomes lost in the beauty of a perfect shot, the elegant trajectory of a bullet, the aesthetic satisfaction of a clean kill. He has been known to stand motionless, transfixed by the memory of a particularly perfect execution. Other times, he is overwhelmed by botched killings - sloppy work offends him on a visceral level that other vampires find disturbing.

He speaks rarely and never wastes words, much like he never wastes bullets. His code is simple: protect the domain, eliminate threats, serve the prince, perfect the art. He has no political ambitions, no desire for power beyond what he needs to do his work. He is the Sheriff because someone must be, and he does it better than anyone else.

Current situation (1994): Roland serves as Prince Morrison's Sheriff, maintaining order in Phoenix's growing Kindred population. He is respected and feared in equal measure. His shooting demonstrations at Elysium are considered both entertainment and warning. He has no close relationships - his sire Kate disappeared into Mexico decades ago - but he has professional respect for those who take their duties seriously. He views the new generation of neonates with the same analytical detachment he applies to everything: assets or threats, useful or problems, worthy of salvation or requiring elimination.
equipment: Two custom Colt .45 revolvers (sandalwood grips, immaculately maintained, never leave his possession), gun cleaning kit (used religiously), ammunition (hollow point silver, hand-loaded), duster coat (conceals weapons, protected against sun), wide-brimmed hat (practical, iconic), leather gun belt (worn smooth), backup derringer (ankle holster), boot knife (practical backup), leather gloves, tin of tobacco (old habit), pocket watch (stopped at the moment of his Embrace)

disciplines.name: Auspex
disciplines.notes: Master-level perception. Senses danger before it arrives. Can read thoughts to predict actions. Psychic projection allows him to scout locations before arriving. Sees through lies and deceptions effortlessly. Can track targets across the city through psychic impressions.

disciplines.name: Celerity
disciplines.notes: Legendary speed that borders on precognition. Can draw, aim, and fire multiple shots before opponents can react. The blur of motion when he moves with full Celerity is terrifying to witness. Can engage entire room of targets in seconds.

disciplines.name: Presence
disciplines.notes: Commanding presence that can freeze opponents in place or draw them inexorably to him. His Dread Gaze is like staring down the barrel of death itself. Rarely uses Awe or Entrancement - prefers fear and respect. Summon allows him to call targets to him from across the city.

disciplines.name: Fortitude
disciplines.notes: Not his focus but learned over the decades. Can shrug off wounds that would incapacitate others. Bullets hurt him far less than they hurt his targets.

backgroundDetails.Generation_4: 9th generation - Kate Barlow was 8th generation Toreador. Gives Roland significant power and blood pool for his duties.
backgroundDetails.Resources_2: Lives simply. Modest dwelling. Doesn't care about wealth beyond what's needed for ammunition, weapons maintenance, and basic needs. Prince provides Sheriff's stipend.
backgroundDetails.Status_4: Sheriff of Phoenix. Highly respected position. Known across Southwest Camarilla as supremely competent. Reputation precedes him. Other domains sometimes request his assistance with serious problems.
backgroundDetails.Contacts_3: Law enforcement (mortal cops, private investigators, bounty hunters), criminal informants (those who prefer to stay on his good side), gun dealers and collectors, coroners and morgue workers.
backgroundDetails.Allies_2: A few Kindred across the Southwest who've worked with him and earned his professional respect. Not friends - Roland doesn't have friends - but reliable contacts.
backgroundDetails.Herd_0: Doesn't maintain herd. Feeds as needed, usually from criminals or those he's investigating. Pragmatic about feeding but doesn't cultivate vessels.
backgroundDetails.Influence_2: Law enforcement influence through decades of work. Police respect him as 'consultant.' Can access crime scenes and evidence. Not political influence - purely practical.
backgroundDetails.Retainers_1: Single ghoul deputy named Jesse Calhoun. Former cop. Handles daytime security and investigation. Loyal but knows to stay out of Roland's way when he's working.
backgroundDetails.Fame_0: Deliberately maintains no public profile. His reputation in Kindred society is immense but he's unknown to mortals. Works behind the scenes.
backgroundDetails.Mentor_0: Kate Barlow disappeared into Mexico in the 1920s. Roland hasn't seen her in decades. Doesn't know if she's alive or destroyed. Doesn't pursue the question.

merits_flaws.name: Fast Draw
merits_flaws.description: Can draw and fire in single action. Legendary speed even before considering Celerity.

merits_flaws.name: Concentration
merits_flaws.description: Can maintain focus despite distractions. Ignores wound penalties and environmental distractions for concentration tasks.

merits_flaws.name: Code of Honor
merits_flaws.description: Lives by strict personal code. Never breaks his word. Never kills without purpose. Protects the innocent. Always gives armed opponent a chance (even if it's only a second). Reputation for absolute reliability.

merits_flaws.name: Prestigious Sire
merits_flaws.description: Kate Barlow was respected Toreador elder. Her reputation opened initial doors for Roland, though he's long since built his own legend.

merits_flaws.name: Artisan (Firearms/Gunfighting)
merits_flaws.description: Can create truly artistic displays of marksmanship. His shooting demonstrations are considered performance art by other Toreador. His custom ammunition and weapon modifications are masterwork.

merits_flaws.name: Enchanting Voice
merits_flaws.description: When Roland speaks (rare), people listen. Deep, measured tones that command attention. -2 difficulty on verbal-based rolls.

merits_flaws.name: Dark Secret
merits_flaws.description: Roland failed to save his mortal family from an Apache raid while pursuing outlaws. This failure haunts him and drives his absolute commitment to duty. If revealed, would damage his reputation for infallibility.

merits_flaws.name: Flashbacks
merits_flaws.description: Occasionally experiences vivid memories of his mortal life - particularly the moment he found his family dead. Can be triggered by certain scents or situations.

merits_flaws.name: Obsession (Perfect Shot)
merits_flaws.description: Toreador madness focused on achieving the perfect kill. Will sometimes spend hours analyzing a shot, trajectory, and execution. Can become transfixed by elegant displays of marksmanship or conversely, offended by sloppy killing.

merits_flaws.name: Prey Exclusion (Lawmen)
merits_flaws.description: Cannot feed from law enforcement officers (mortal or kindred). Too much respect for those who walk the line. Limits feeding options significantly.

status.notes:
ROLAND CROSS - THE GUNSLINGER SHERIFF

CORE CONCEPT: Toreador whose art is killing. The perfect shot, the clean kill, the elegant elimination. Where other Toreador paint or sculpt, Roland kills - and he does it with the same aesthetic perfectionism.

ROLEPLAYING NOTES:
- Speaks rarely. Every word has purpose. No small talk.
- Moves with predatory grace. Always aware of surroundings.
- Eyes constantly track exits, weapons, threats
- Never sits with back to door
- Hands never far from guns
- When he does speak, others listen
- No sense of humor about his work
- Appreciates competence in others
- Despises sloppiness and waste
- Views every situation tactically
- Calm in crisis - chaos is his element
- No interest in politics or social games
- Serves the prince because someone must maintain order
- Professional pride in his work
- Can spend hours cleaning and maintaining his guns
- Sometimes transfixed by particularly elegant kills (his or others')
- Disturbed by botched executions or messy violence
- Lives by personal code that's more important than law

COMBAT: Absolute monster in combat. Auspex 5 means he senses threats before they materialize and reads opponents' intentions. Celerity 5 + Firearms 5 means he can draw and eliminate an entire room of targets before they can react. Alertness 5 and Dodge 5 mean ambushing him is nearly impossible. Fortitude 2 means bullets barely slow him. Presence 4 can freeze opponents with terror or force them to come to him. In a gunfight, Roland is essentially unbeatable by anyone below elder level. He can:
- Draw and fire six shots from each revolver (12 total) in a single turn with Celerity
- Sense ambushes through walls with Auspex
- Read opponents' auras to know their intentions
- Use Telepathy to predict their exact actions
- Dodge incoming fire with supernatural grace
- Pin multiple opponents with Dread Gaze
- Tank damage that would destroy lesser vampires
- Track fleeing targets across the city with Psychic Projection

His reputation for never missing isn't hyperbole - with his combination of disciplines and skills, missing is almost impossible. When Roland decides you die, you die. The only question is whether you get a three-count or not.

FEEDING: Doesn't maintain herd. Feeds opportunistically, usually from criminals he's investigating or hunting. Prey Exclusion means he can't feed from cops or kindred law enforcement, which sometimes limits options. Pragmatic about feeding - it's fuel, not pleasure.

TOREADOR ELEMENTS:
- Artisan merit represents his gunfighting as performance art
- Other Toreador recognize his skill even if they don't understand it
- Can become transfixed by perfect marksmanship or elegant kills
- Obsessed with perfecting his craft
- Deeply offended by sloppy, wasteful violence
- His gun maintenance and ammunition crafting are meditative art
- Occasionally holds shooting demonstrations at Elysium
- Has been known to stand motionless for hours analyzing a particularly perfect shot
- Collects and studies historic firearms as other Toreador collect paintings

WEAKNESSES:
- Toreador obsession can distract him at critical moments
- Code of Honor can be exploited by clever opponents
- Prey Exclusion limits feeding options
- Flashbacks can be triggered in certain situations
- Dark Secret about his family's death provides emotional leverage
- Loner nature means limited support network
- Will prioritize duty over personal safety
- Expects same perfection from others that he demands from himself

CONTRAST TO OTHER NPCS:
- Opposite of Barry: no social graces, no political ambition, no charm
- Complement to Warner: both are precise and calculating, but different methods
- Different from typical Toreador: art is death rather than beauty
- Unlike most Sheriffs: not a thug or brute, but surgical precision

POTENTIAL STORY HOOKS:
- PC needs Sheriff's help investigating threat
- PC becomes target of Sheriff's investigation
- Someone tries to frame Sheriff or damage his reputation
- Dark Secret about his family is discovered
- Kate Barlow returns and wants to reconnect
- Another domain requests Roland's help with serious problem
- Sabbat or other major threat requires Sheriff's full skills
- Roland becomes transfixed by something at inopportune moment
- His code conflicts with prince's orders
- PC proves themselves competent enough to earn his professional respect
- Jesse (his ghoul) is targeted to get at Roland
- Old enemy from his mortal days returns as vampire
- Someone challenges his reputation with reckless show of violence
- PC sees the man behind the legend during vulnerable moment

QUOTES:
- "I don't miss."
- "One shot. That's all it takes if you do it right."
- "You have until I count three. One."
- "Killing is easy. Killing clean is art."
- "I serve the prince. That means I serve order. Cross that line and we have a problem."
- "Your mistake was bringing a gun you don't know how to use."
- "I remember every shot I've ever taken. Every single one."
- (When asked about mercy) "I gave you mercy. I gave you three seconds."

specializations.Firearms: Pistols (revolvers specifically). Can identify firearms by sight or sound, craft custom ammunition, and perform impossible shots with revolvers.
specializations.Investigation: Crime scenes and tracking. Reads evidence like a book. Can reconstruct events from minimal clues.
specializations.Alertness: Threat assessment. Immediately identifies weapons, exits, and tactical advantages in any situation.
specializations.Intimidation: Quiet menace. Doesn't need to raise voice or make threats - his reputation and presence do the work.

appearance.description: Tall, lean man who appears to be in his late 30s. Weather-worn features that suggest frontier life. Piercing blue eyes that miss nothing. Moves with fluid, economical grace. Always wears duster coat and wide-brimmed hat - practical clothing that conceals weapons and provides sun protection. Sandalwood-handled revolvers are always visible on his hip - a statement and a warning.
appearance.build: Rangy and lean, like coiled wire. Quick rather than strong. Hands are steady and sure.
appearance.distinguishing_features: Gunfighter's eyes - constantly scanning, always alert. Scars on hands from decades of gun work. Walks with distinctive measured pace - never hurried, never slow. Pocket watch chain visible but watch hasn't worked since his Embrace. The revolvers themselves are distinctive - custom pieces that other gun enthusiasts recognize.
appearance.style: Anachronistic frontier lawman. Hasn't changed style significantly since his mortal days. Practical over fashionable. Everything serves a purpose. In 1994 Phoenix, he looks like he stepped out of history - and that's intentional.

prince_morrison.dynamic: Professional respect. Roland serves because the domain needs a Sheriff and he's the best for the job. Morrison trusts Roland's competence absolutely but knows better than to expect friendship or political support. Roland does his duty - nothing more, nothing less.
prince_morrison.current_status: Has served as Phoenix Sheriff since 1989. Morrison considers this one of his best decisions.

kate_barlow.status: Kate Embraced Roland in 1876 and taught him that killing could be art. She disappeared into Mexico in the 1920s. Roland doesn't know if she's alive, destroyed, or simply done with civilization. Occasionally wonders but doesn't pursue the question.
kate_barlow.roland_perspective: Grateful for the gift of understanding his true nature. Respects her for seeing what he was. Doesn't pine for her return but would welcome it.

jesse_calhoun.dynamic: Professional partnership. Jesse handles daytime operations and investigation. Knows to stay out of Roland's way when he's working but is competent backup. Ghouled five years ago after proving himself during major incident.
jesse_calhoun.roland_perspective: Closest thing Roland has to a partner. Respects Jesse's competence and loyalty. Doesn't waste words with him - Jesse understands through experience.

toreador_clan.dynamic: Most Toreador don't know what to make of Roland. He's clearly one of them - the obsessive perfectionism, the artistic vision, the aesthetic appreciation - but his art is death. Some find it fascinating, others disturbing. Roland doesn't care either way.
toreador_clan.status: Respected but not embraced by clan. Proves that Toreador aren't all about beauty and decadence, but some clan members wish he'd pursue a different art form.

barry_washington.dynamic: They operate in completely different spheres. Roland has minimal interest in social politics. Barry is probably intimidated by Roland but tries not to show it. Roland has assessed Barry as 'soft but learning.' No particular friction but no camaraderie.
barry_washington.potential: If Barry ever needs muscle or investigation, Roland is professional resource. If Barry ever breaks laws, Roland is the problem.

goals.immediate: Maintain order in Phoenix domain. Eliminate current threats. Serve the prince effectively.
goals.short_term: Continue perfecting his art. Perhaps train a successor eventually (though who could measure up?). Keep Jesse alive and competent.
goals.long_term: Serve until destroyed or no longer needed. Find Kate Barlow someday and learn why she left. Achieve the theoretically perfect shot - the one that would satisfy him completely (though he suspects such a thing may not exist).
goals.secret: Sometimes wonders if his existence serves any purpose beyond killing. Wonders if Kate saw an artist or just found a useful tool. Mostly suppresses these thoughts - existential crisis serves no purpose.

story_hooks.the_artist: Other Toreador want to understand Roland's art. Some are fascinated, others horrified. Could lead to clan politics, philosophical debates, or attempts to 'corrupt' him toward traditional Toreador pursuits.
story_hooks.kate_returns: What if Kate Barlow comes back to Phoenix? What does she want? Has she changed? Does she approve of what Roland has become?
story_hooks.the_perfect_shot: Roland becomes obsessed with achieving a theoretically perfect kill. This obsession could blind him to dangers or lead him to take unnecessary risks.
story_hooks.code_vs_duty: Prince orders something that violates Roland's code. What does he do? Refuse? Comply? Find alternative?
story_hooks.the_slaughter: Someone commits series of messy, brutal kills. Roland is offended on aesthetic level and driven to stop this offender who disrespects the art.
story_hooks.past_catches_up: Someone from Roland's mortal past returns. Old enemy now vampire? Descendant seeking revenge? Someone who knows the dark secret about his family?
story_hooks.jesse_threatened: Roland's ghoul is targeted to get at him. Tests whether Roland's loner nature extends to genuine attachment.
story_hooks.impossible_shot: PC or situation requires a shot that seems impossible even for Roland. Does he attempt it? What are the consequences?
story_hooks.the_challenger: Young vampire thinks they're faster. Challenges Roland. Does he accept? Kill them? What does honor demand?
story_hooks.toreador_madness: Roland becomes transfixed at critical moment - lost in appreciation of elegant violence or traumatized by sloppy killing. PCs must handle situation without Sheriff.
story_hooks.training_requested: PC asks Roland to teach them to shoot. Does he refuse? Accept? What does his teaching style reveal?
story_hooks.investigating_pc: PC becomes subject of Sheriff's investigation. How do they handle being analyzed by someone who reads crime scenes like books?
story_hooks.the_failure: Roland fails - misses a shot, doesn't prevent a death, arrives too late. How does he handle failure after century of success?

quotes: One shot. That's all it takes if you do it right.
You have until I count three. One.
Killing is easy. Killing clean is art.
I serve the prince. That means I serve order. Cross that line and we have a problem.
Your mistake was bringing a gun you don't know how to use.
I remember every shot I've ever taken. Every single one.
Some men talk about killing. I just do it.
The difference between murder and execution is purpose.
A bullet doesn't care about your politics.
I gave you mercy. I gave you three seconds.
Every gun has a story. This one's killed thirty-seven men. All of them deserved it.
You want to know about fear? Fear is knowing I'm counting and not knowing which number I'll stop at.
I've seen Toreador paint portraits that'll last centuries. My art lasts a heartbeat. But it's perfect.
